response.justification: The candidate has approximately 17 years of broad engineering experience and genuine entrepreneurial credentials, but falls significantly short of the role's core requirement: recognized, demonstrated AI expertise. AI-related work (MCP tooling, agentic workflows) appears only in the most recent role beginning April 2025, with no prior record of building or shipping AI-powered products or platforms at scale. The position is a Director-level AI strategy role requiring established expertise in advising customers and partners on cloud and AI adoption; the candidate's profile reflects strong individual technical execution across web, backend, and infrastructure domains, but shows no evidence of strategic advisory influence, external thought leadership, or the customer-facing AI authority this role demands.
strengths: 17+ years of diverse engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-performance backend systems — comfortably clears the 15-year tenure requirement
Genuine founder and entrepreneurial experience with GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform actively built and maintained over nine years
Demonstrated technical breadth including cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) and recent hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code-generation frameworks
concerns: AI expertise is limited to under one year of recent work with no prior record of building AI-powered products at scale, far short of the 'recognized expertise with demonstrated AI products or platforms' the role requires
No evidence of Director-level or strategic advisory experience — the role requires advising customers and partners on AI and cloud adoption strategy, which is absent from the candidate's history
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and no indicators of external recognition as an AI expert (publications, keynotes, industry awards or analyst citations)

key_factors.factor: AI expertise depth and track record
